TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_nipaluma-alupal,_alopal, title = nipaluma-alupal,_alopal, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/nipaluma-alupal,_alopal} }

S.FORM: == alupal, alopal == Êüø kagak kakuniza subal :ÁúüÊÝ∏Âüü Eukaryota salaedan :Ê§çÁâ©Áïå Plantae panan :Ë¢´Â≠êÊ§çÁâ©ÈñÄ Magnoliophyta upiz :ÈõôÂ≠êËëâÊ§çÁâ©Á∂± Magnoliopsida mata :ÊüøÊ®πÁõÆ Ericales sapamat :ÊüøÊ®πÁßë Ebenaceae mikitinay :ÊüøÊ®πÂ±¨ Diospyros hicahicaay : u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika The persimmon /p…ôr's…™m…ôn/ (sometimes spelled persimon) is the edible fruit of a number of species of trees in the genus Diospyros. The most widely cultivated of these is the Oriental or Japanese persimmon, Diospyros kaki. Diospyros is in the family Ebenaceae, and a number of non-persimmon species of the genus are grown for ebony timber.
S.PHON: == ar~…æupar~…æ, ar~…æopar~…æ == * kagak kakuniza subar~…æ :*** …ôukarjota sar~…æa…ôdan :*** pr~…æanta…ô panan :***** magnor~…æiopƒßjta upiz :****** magnor~…æiopsida mata :*** …ôri ¶ar~…æ…ôs sapamat :*** …ôb…ôna ¶…ôa…ô mikitinaj :*** diospjros ƒßi ¶aƒßi ¶aaj : u siwkaj nu nipar~…æuma u san…ôk nu nipar~…æuma mar~…æaƒß…ô ¶i tu im…ôr~…æa≈ã pasubana i ¶a ¶udadan a r~…æa ¶ur~…æ u zuma a ¶id…ôkaj a kamu, sau≈ãaj misapaju î~ ° a ¶id…ôkaj u sur~…æit nu zuma a kamu-ƒßur~…æam atu amir~…æika tƒß…ô p…ôrsimmon /p…ôr î~ °s*m…ôn/ (som…ôtim…ôs sp…ôr~…ær~…æ…ôd p…ôrsimon) is tƒß…ô …ôdibr~…æ…ô fruit of a numb…ôr of sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tr…ô…ôs in tƒß…ô g…ônus diospjros. tƒß…ô most wid…ôr~…æj ¶ur~…æti*at…ôd of tƒß…ôs…ô is tƒß…ô ori…ôntar~…æ or *apan…ôs…ô p…ôrsimmon, diospjros kaki. diospjros is in tƒß…ô famir~…æj …ôb…ôna ¶…ôa…ô, and a numb…ôr of non-p…ôrsimmon sp…ô ¶i…ôs of tƒß…ô g…ônus ar…ô grown for …ôbonj timb…ôr.
S.FORM: == alupal, alopal == Êüø kagak kakuniza subal :ÁúüÊÝ∏Âüü Eukaryota salaedan :Ê§çÁâ©Áïå Plantae panan :Ë¢´Â≠êÊ§çÁâ©ÈñÄ Magnoliophyta upiz :ÈõôÂ≠êËëâÊ§çÁâ©Á∂± Magnoliopsida mata :ÊüøÊ®πÁõÆ Ericales sapamat :ÊüøÊ®πÁßë Ebenaceae mikitinay :ÊüøÊ®πÂ±¨ Diospyros hicahicaay : u siwkay nu nipaluma u sanek nu nipaluma malaheci tu imelang pasubana i cacudadan a lacul u zuma a cidekay a kamu, saungay misapayu' a cidekay u sulit nu zuma a kamu-Hulam atu Amilika The persimmon /p…ôr's…™m…ôn/ (sometimes spelled persimon) is the edible fruit of a number of species of trees in the genus Diospyros. The most widely cultivated of these is the Oriental or Japanese persimmon, Diospyros kaki. Diospyros is in the family Ebenaceae, and a number of non-persimmon species of the genus are grown for ebony timber.
